title: Llama7B quality-backed KV8 measured-compute substitution
hypothesis: When the Llama7B 131k physical-HBM memory/NoC model uses measured corrected NPU compute PPA instead of abstract MAC/cycle points, the frontier will move from optimistic HBM dominance toward a die-budgeted balance between compute replicas, SRAM allocation, and HBM service.

direct_comparison.primary_question: For quality-backed native-GQA KV8 at 131k context, which measured nm1..nm64 compute block and replica budget is feasible under practical die-area splits, and does the selected point remain memory-bound?
include: merged corrected compute PPA rows for nm1, nm2, nm4, nm8, nm16, nm32, and nm64
flat and hierarchical rows where status=ok
best practical HBM service from the memory/NoC frontier: 8 stacks, 16 pseudo-channels per stack, 64-bit pseudo-channels, 9000 MT/s, 0.75 efficiency

expected_benefit: prevents the Llama7B architecture ranking from using physically ungrounded compute throughput
identifies whether many small measured compute blocks beat one wide block under die budgets
selects the next RTL/PPA target for hierarchical compute composition or memory hierarchy refinement
risks: vector throughput for attention softmax is still a ratio assumption tied to MAC throughput
NoC and SRAM are service models rather than detailed physical implementations
measured compute rows are block-level PPA and do not include full tiled-dispatch overhead

required_evaluations.objective: Run the quality-backed native-GQA KV8 131k Llama7B physical-HBM model with measured corrected NPU compute PPA substituted for abstract MAC/cycle points.

expected_result.reason: If measured compute makes the frontier compute-bound, schedule hierarchical/tiled compute and dispatcher/NoC work; if it remains HBM-bound, continue memory hierarchy/HBM service refinement.
